tools.name: listCustomers
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of customers, with options for searching, filtering by ID, external ID, email, phone number, communication status, auto-charge status, creation date, customer name, and business entity ID.

args.name: cursor
args.description: The cursor for pagination to fetch the next or previous set of records.

args.name: limit
args.description: Maximum number of customers to return per page (defaults to 10).

args.name: search
args.description: Advanced key-value search query. Format: 'field=value' or 'field1=value1,field2=value2'. Supports any customer field including nested JSON paths. Examples: 'customer_name=Acme', 'email=john@example.com', 'customer_name=Acme,external_id=EXT-100', 'custom_data.users=abc@dev.in'. String fields use case-insensitive contains matching. Do NOT pass plain text — always use field=value format.

args.name: search_name_external_id
args.description: Quick relevance-ranked search across customer_name and external_id. Accepts plain text (e.g., 'Acme'). Results are ranked: exact match first, then starts-with, then contains. Simpler alternative to the 'search' parameter when you only need to find customers by name or external ID.

args.name: order
args.description: Order of results (e.g., '-created_at' for descending creation date).

args.name: sort_key
args.description: Key to sort the results by (e.g., 'id').

args.name: sort_type
args.description: Sort order type (e.g., 'asc' for ascending).

args.name: expand
args.description: Fields to expand in the response (e.g., 'contacts').

args.name: id
args.description: Filter by customer ID (exact match).

args.name: id__in
args.description: Filter by a comma-separated list of customer IDs.

args.name: external_id
args.description: Filter by external ID (exact match).

args.name: external_id__ilike
args.description: Case-insensitive partial match for external ID.

args.name: email
args.description: Filter by email (exact match).

args.name: email__ilike
args.description: Case-insensitive partial match for email.

args.name: phone_number__ilike
args.description: Case-insensitive partial match for phone number.

args.name: communications_enabled
args.description: Filter by communications enabled status.

args.name: auto_charge_enabled
args.description: Filter by auto-charge enabled status.

args.name: created_at__gte
args.description: Filter by creation date greater than or equal to (ISO 8601 format).

args.name: created_at__lte
args.description: Filter by creation date less than or equal to (ISO 8601 format).

args.name: customer_name__ilike
args.description: Case-insensitive partial match for customer name.

args.name: business_entity_id
args.description: Filter by business entity ID (exact match).

args.name: invoice__customer_id
args.description: Filter by invoice customer ID.

args.name: invoice__status
args.description: Filter by invoice status.

responseTemplate.prependBody:
## Customer List

Here is the list of customers based on your criteria:

args.name: customerId

responseTemplate.prependBody:

Here are the details for the requested customer:

tools.name: listInvoices
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of invoices, with options for filtering by customer, status, amounts, due date, creation date, and related customer/contract details.

args.name: customer_id
args.description: Filter invoices by a specific customer ID.

args.name: customer__external_id
args.description: Filter invoices by a customer's external ID (nested filter).

args.name: status
args.description: Filter invoices by their status (e.g., 'draft', 'approved', 'paid', 'void', 'partially_paid', 'upcoming').

args.name: status__in
args.description: Filter invoices by multiple statuses (comma-separated, e.g., 'draft,approved,paid,partially_paid,upcoming,void').

args.name: cursor
args.description: The cursor for pagination.

args.name: limit
args.description: Maximum number of invoices to return per page (defaults to 10).

args.name: contract_id
args.description: Filter by contract ID.

args.name: external_id
args.description: Filter by invoice external ID.

args.name: invoice_total__gte
args.description: Filter by invoice total greater than or equal to.

args.name: invoice_total__lte
args.description: Filter by invoice total less than or equal to.

args.name: amount_due__gte
args.description: Filter by amount due greater than or equal to.

args.name: amount_due__lte
args.description: Filter by amount due less than or equal to.

args.name: due_date__gte
args.description: Filter by due date greater than or equal to (ISO 8601).

args.name: due_date__lte
args.description: Filter by due date less than or equal to (ISO 8601).

args.name: created_at__gte
args.description: Filter by creation date greater than or equal to (ISO 8601).

args.name: created_at__lte
args.description: Filter by creation date less than or equal to (ISO 8601).

args.name: invoice_number__like
args.description: Partial match for invoice number.

args.name: customer__customer_name__ilike
args.description: Case-insensitive partial match for customer name associated with the invoice.

responseTemplate.prependBody:
## Invoice List

Here are the invoices matching your criteria:

tools.name: listAggregates
tools.description: List Billable Metrics (also called aggregates in backend/API terminology) in paginated form, with filtering by ID, name, and data source.

args.name: cursor

args.name: limit
args.description: Maximum number of records to return per page (defaults to 10).

args.name: order
args.description: Order of results (e.g., '-created_at').

args.name: sort_key
args.description: Key to sort the results by (e.g., 'id').

args.name: sort_type
args.description: Sort order type (e.g., 'asc').

args.name: id
args.description: Filter by aggregate ID.

args.name: name__ilike

responseTemplate.prependBody:

Here is the list of billable metrics. Backend/API terminology may also call these aggregates:

tools.name: listRawMetrics
tools.description: List Usage Events. In backend/API terminology these are also called raw metrics. Supports filtering by ID, name, and API slug.

args.name: cursor
args.description: The cursor for pagination.

args.name: limit
args.description: Maximum number of records to return per page (defaults to 10).

args.name: order
args.description: Order of results (e.g., '-created_at').

args.name: sort_key
args.description: Key to sort the results by (e.g., 'created_at').

args.name: sort_type
args.description: Sort order type (e.g., 'asc').

args.name: search
args.description: General search query.

args.name: id
args.description: Filter by usage event ID.

args.name: name__ilike
args.description: Case-insensitive partial match for usage event name.

args.name: api_slug__ilike
args.description: Case-insensitive partial match for API slug.

responseTemplate.prependBody:
## Usage Events

Here is the list of usage events. Backend/API terminology may also call these raw metrics:

tools.name: createRawMetric
tools.description: Create a new Usage Event schema. In backend/API terminology this resource is called a raw metric. This defines the schema used for ingesting usage events.

approvalConfig.title: Create Usage Event Schema
approvalConfig.description: This will create a new Usage Event schema (called a raw metric in backend/API terminology) and provision its event schema. Please review the payload carefully.
approvalConfig.warningText: Ensure the dataschema and slug values are correct before continuing.

args.name: name
args.description: Human-friendly name for the Usage Event schema (raw metric).

args.name: api_slug
args.description: API slug used when ingesting Usage Events (lowercase, underscores).

args.name: dataschema
args.description: Data schema of the Usage Event resource (called a raw metric in backend/API terminology). Structure: {"data": {custom_fields}, "timestamp": "DateTime64", "customer_id": "String"}. The 'timestamp' and 'customer_id' fields are mandatory schema fields for usage ingestion. The 'data' map must include only valid ClickHouse types (String, Int64, Float64, Date32, DateTime64, UUID, Bool); use 'Bool' instead of 'Boolean'.

args.name: column_order
args.description: Optional ordered list of columns for downstream processing. Locked to ['timestamp'] in production; leave as-is unless backend explicitly requests a schema change.

responseTemplate.prependBody:
## Usage Event Schema Created

The Usage Event schema (raw metric) was created successfully:

tools.name: getRawMetricById
tools.description: Get Usage Event details by ID. Backend/API terminology may also call this a raw metric.

args.name: rawMetricId
args.description: The ID of the Usage Event resource (raw metric).

responseTemplate.prependBody:
## Usage Event Details

Here are the details for the specified usage event:

tools.name: getRawMetricLogs
tools.description: Get Usage Event event rows for a specific resource by ID. This follows the same visual-preview query path used by the frontend meter page, so it returns recent event rows rather than only backend sample logs. Backend/API terminology may also call this a raw metric.

args.name: rawMetricId
args.description: The ID of the Usage Event resource (raw metric).

args.name: filters
args.description: Optional visual-query filter array, matching the frontend usage-event viewer format. Default is an empty filter list.

args.name: limit
args.description: Maximum number of event rows to return. Defaults to 20, matching frontend behavior.

args.name: offset
args.description: Pagination offset for event rows. Defaults to 0.

args.name: aggregate_operation
args.description: Optional aggregate operation object used by the frontend for event counts (for example COUNT on timestamp). Leave null for normal row listing.

args.name: order_by
args.description: Optional ordering array. Defaults to [{column:'timestamp', type:'DESC'}] to match frontend event viewing.

responseTemplate.prependBody:
## Usage Event Logs

Here are recent event rows for the specified usage event:

tools.name: getRawMetricBySlug
tools.description: Get Usage Event details by API slug. Backend/API terminology may also call this a raw metric.

args.name: rawMetricSlug
args.description: The API slug of the Usage Event resource (raw metric).

responseTemplate.prependBody:
## Usage Event by Slug

Here are the details for the usage event with the specified slug:

tools.name: createEntitlement
tools.description: Creates a new entitlement in the system with various attributes including name, description, type, units, and product association.

approvalConfig.title: Create New Entitlement
approvalConfig.description: This will create a new entitlement in your system. Please review the details carefully.
approvalConfig.warningText: This action will create a new entitlement record that may affect billing and access control.

args.name: name
args.description: Entitlement name (required).

args.name: description
args.description: Entitlement description (optional).

args.name: entitlement_type
args.description: Type of entitlement (e.g., 'Quantity', 'Feature', 'Access').

args.name: units
args.description: Units for the entitlement (e.g., 'units', 'GB', 'users').

args.name: is_active
args.description: Whether the entitlement is active (defaults to true).

args.name: product_id
args.description: Associated product ID (UUID format, optional).

responseTemplate.prependBody:
## Entitlement Created

New entitlement has been successfully created:

tools.name: approveInvoice
tools.description: Approves an invoice. This changes the invoice status to approved and may trigger billing processes.

approvalConfig.title: Approve Invoice
approvalConfig.description: This will approve the specified invoice without sending email notification. Please review the invoice details carefully.
approvalConfig.warningText: This action will approve the invoice and may trigger billing processes. This cannot be easily undone.

args.name: invoiceId
args.description: The unique ID of the invoice to approve (required).

args.name: send_email
args.description: Whether to send email notification (defaults to false).

args.name: check_duplicate_invoice
args.description: Whether to check for duplicate invoices (defaults to true).

responseTemplate.prependBody:
## Invoice Approved

The invoice has been successfully approved:

tools.name: recogniseRevenue
tools.description: Recognizes revenue for the organization up to a specified end date. This triggers revenue recognition processing for all eligible transactions.

approvalConfig.title: Recognise Revenue
approvalConfig.description: This will trigger revenue recognition processing up to the specified end date. Please review the date carefully.
approvalConfig.warningText: This action will process revenue recognition which may affect financial reporting and accounting records.

args.name: end_date
args.description: End date for revenue recognition in YYYY-MM-DD format (required).

responseTemplate.prependBody:
## Revenue Recognition

Revenue recognition has been processed successfully:

args.description: Business Entity Name (required).

args.name: email
args.description: Business Entity email address (optional).

args.name: phone_number
args.description: Business Entity phone number with country code (optional).

args.name: is_default
args.description: Whether this is the default business entity (defaults to false).

args.name: logo_url
args.description: Business Entity logo URL (optional).

args.name: tax_config
args.description: Business Entity tax configuration as JSON object (optional).

args.name: address_line1
args.description: Address line 1 (optional).

args.name: address_line2
args.description: Address line 2 (optional).

args.name: address_line3
args.description: Address line 3 (optional).

args.name: address_city
args.description: Address city (optional).

args.name: address_state
args.description: Address state (optional).

args.name: address_zipCode
args.description: Address zip code (optional).

args.name: address_country
args.description: Address country (optional).

responseTemplate.prependBody:
## Business Entity Created

New business entity has been successfully created:

tools.name: createCustomer
tools.description: Creates a new customer in the system with various attributes including address, tax info, and contact details.

approvalConfig.title: Create New Customer
approvalConfig.description: This will create a new customer in your system. Please review the details carefully.
approvalConfig.warningText: This action will create a new customer record in your system.

args.name: external_id
args.description: External customer ID (optional).

args.name: customer_name
args.description: Customer name (required).

args.name: email
args.description: Customer primary email address (optional).

args.name: phone_number
args.description: Customer phone number (optional).

args.name: address_line1
args.description: Address line 1 (optional).

args.name: address_line2
args.description: Address line 2 (optional).

args.name: address_line3
args.description: Address line 3 (optional).

args.name: address_city
args.description: Address city (optional).

args.name: address_state
args.description: Address state (optional).

args.name: address_zipCode
args.description: Address zip code (optional).

args.name: address_country
args.description: Address country (optional).

args.name: address_country_code
args.description: Address country code (optional).

args.name: ship_to_line1
args.description: Shipping address line 1 (optional).

args.name: ship_to_line2
args.description: Shipping address line 2 (optional).

args.name: ship_to_line3
args.description: Shipping address line 3 (optional).

args.name: ship_to_city
args.description: Shipping address city (optional).

args.name: ship_to_state
args.description: Shipping address state (optional).

args.name: ship_to_zipCode
args.description: Shipping address zip code (optional).

args.name: ship_to_country
args.description: Shipping address country (optional).

args.name: ship_to_country_code
args.description: Shipping address country code (optional).

args.name: communications_enabled
args.description: Enable/disable communications (defaults to true).

args.name: auto_charge_enabled
args.description: Enable/disable auto-charge (defaults to true).

args.name: business_entity_id
args.description: Business entity ID (UUID format, optional).

responseTemplate.prependBody:
## Customer Created

New customer has been successfully created:

tools.name: ingestRawMetricEvent
tools.description: Ingest a Usage Event for the specified resource slug. Backend/API terminology may also call the target resource a raw metric.

approvalConfig.title: Ingest Usage Event
approvalConfig.description: This will ingest a Usage Event for the selected Usage Event schema (called a raw metric in backend/API terminology). Review the payload carefully.
approvalConfig.warningText: Ensure the data payload matches the Usage Event schema (raw metric schema) before proceeding.

args.name: rawMetricSlug
args.description: Slug of the Usage Event resource (raw metric), e.g. api_usage_events.

args.name: event
args.description: Usage Event payload containing customer_id, timestamp, and a data map with schema-compliant types. customer_id and timestamp are mandatory event fields and must be provided in the payload; MCP does not auto-add them. Before constructing the event, fetch the schema via getRawMetricBySlug (or reuse the response from createRawMetric) so you only include fields that exist in the Usage Event schema (raw metric schema). Use the provided type buckets such as String/Int64 or the exact column names returned by sample logs; do not invent new keys.

responseTemplate.prependBody:
## Usage Event Ingested

The usage event was ingested successfully:

tools.name: extractContractFromRaw
tools.description: Extract structured contract data from raw text content using AI. This tool analyzes natural language contract descriptions and extracts key fields like dates, products, pricing, and billing terms. The extracted data can then be used to create a customer and contract via createCustomer and createContract tools.

args.name: content
args.description: The raw text content describing the contract (e.g., 'Contract starts on 1st Jan 2025 and ends on 31 Dec 2025 with a single product called chat subscription, cost is $10 per month prepaid.').

args.name: name
args.description: A name for the contract being extracted.

args.name: organization_id
args.description: The organization ID for the contract extraction (will be auto-populated from user context if not provided).

responseTemplate.prependBody:
## Contract Extraction Result

The AI has extracted the following contract data from your raw text. You can use this to create a customer (if missing_fields includes customer info) and then create the contract:

tools.name: createContract
tools.description: Create a new contract in Zenskar. Use this after extracting contract data via extractContractFromRaw and creating a customer via createCustomer. The contract includes customer details, pricing phases, products, and billing configuration.

approvalConfig.title: Create New Contract
approvalConfig.description: This will create a new contract in your system. Please review the contract details carefully including customer, dates, pricing, and billing terms.
approvalConfig.warningText: This action will create a new contract record that may trigger billing processes.

args.name: name
args.description: Contract name (required).

args.name: description
args.description: Contract description (optional).

args.name: status
args.description: Contract status (required, typically 'draft' for new contracts).

args.name: currency
args.description: Currency code for the contract (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

args.name: start_date
args.description: Contract start date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., '2025-01-01T00:00:00.000000').

args.name: end_date
args.description: Contract end date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., '2025-12-31T23:59:59.999999'). Optional — omit for open-ended contracts.

args.name: anchor_date
args.description: Anchor date for billing cycles in ISO 8601 format (defaults to start_date).

args.name: customer_id
args.description: The ID of the customer for this contract (required, UUID format).

args.name: renewal_policy
args.description: Contract renewal policy. Currently only 'do_not_renew' is supported at runtime.

args.name: bill_parent_customer
args.description: Whether to bill the parent customer (defaults to false).

args.name: phases
args.description: Array of contract phases with pricing details. Each phase includes name, start_date, end_date, phase_type, and pricings array. Defaults to empty array if omitted.

args.name: source
args.description: Source information for the contract (e.g., {source_type: 'ai', source_id: 'uuid'}).

args.name: custom_attributes
args.description: Custom attributes for the contract as key-value pairs.

responseTemplate.prependBody:
## Contract Created

New contract has been successfully created:

tools.name: getContractById
tools.description: Retrieve a contract by its ID, including all phases, pricings, customer details, custom attributes, and renewal policy. Essential for cloning contracts for renewals, reviewing terms before amendments, and validating contract state.

args.name: contractId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the contract to retrieve.

tools.name: updateContract
tools.description: Update an existing contract. This is a PUT endpoint — you MUST fetch the contract first with getContractById, then send ALL required fields including phases. Without phases the API returns 500. Copy phases from the GET response (each phase needs name, start_date, end_date at minimum). Status: draft, active, paused, expired, disputed. Renewal: renew_with_default_contract, renew_with_existing, do_not_renew.

args.name: contractId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the contract to update.

args.name: name
args.description: Human-readable name for the contract.

args.name: status
args.description: Contract status: draft, active, paused, or disputed. Cannot set to 'expired' via update.

args.name: currency
args.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code (e.g. USD, EUR, GBP).

args.name: start_date
args.description: Contract start date in ISO 8601 format (e.g. 2026-01-01T00:00:00).

args.name: customer_id
args.description: UUID of the customer associated with this contract.

args.name: description
args.description: Detailed description of the contract terms.

args.name: end_date
args.description: Contract end date in ISO 8601 format.

args.name: anchor_date
args.description: Reference date for billing cycle calculations.

args.name: is_last_day_of_month
args.description: When true, billing cycles always end on the last day of the month.

args.name: custom_attributes
args.description: Key-value pairs for organization-specific metadata (e.g. automatic_renewal flag).

args.name: phases
args.description: Array of contract phases. Each phase needs at minimum: name, start_date, end_date. Include pricings array if the phase has pricing. Defaults to empty array if omitted.

args.name: renewal_policy
args.description: Contract renewal behavior: renew_with_default_contract, renew_with_existing, or do_not_renew.

args.name: contract_link
args.description: URL to external contract document or signed agreement.

args.name: bill_parent_customer
args.description: When true and customer has a parent relationship, invoices are sent to the parent.

args.name: invoice_payer_customer_id
args.description: Optional customer UUID who will receive and pay invoices for this contract.

args.name: include_in_renewal
args.description: When true, this contract is eligible for renewal processes.

args.name: tags
args.description: List of tags for categorizing and filtering contracts.

args.name: source
args.description: Metadata about the originating system (e.g. {source_type: 'hubspot', source_id: 'deal_123'}).

tools.name: deleteContract
tools.description: Delete a contract by its ID. Only works on draft contracts. Returns the deleted contract object with a deleted_at timestamp.

args.name: contractId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the contract to delete.

tools.name: getContractAmendments
tools.description: Retrieve all amendments for a contract. Shows history of changes, expansions, upgrades, and modifications made to the contract over time.

args.name: contractId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the contract.

tools.name: createContractPhase
tools.description: Add a new phase to an existing contract. Use this for add-on expansions, mid-contract changes, or creating distinct billing periods with different pricing. Phase type enum: active, pause, trial.

args.name: contractId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the contract to add a phase to.

args.name: name
args.description: Name for this contract phase (e.g. 'Add-On Phase', 'Expansion Phase').

args.name: start_date
args.description: Phase start date in ISO 8601 format.

args.name: end_date
args.description: Phase end date in ISO 8601 format.

args.name: phase_type
args.description: Type of phase: active, pause, or trial.

args.name: description
args.description: Description of this phase.

args.name: pricings
args.description: Array of pricing configurations for this phase.

args.name: source_plan_phase_id
args.description: UUID of the plan phase template this was derived from.

args.name: phase_metadata
args.description: Additional metadata for this phase as key-value pairs.

tools.name: createContractPhasePricing
tools.description: Add pricing to a contract phase. Either pricing_id (reference existing product pricing) or pricing (inline pricing object) must be provided. Use product_id to associate with a product.

args.name: contractId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the contract.

args.name: phaseId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the phase to add pricing to.

args.name: pricing_id
args.description: UUID of an existing product pricing to reference. Either this or inline pricing must be provided.

args.name: product_id
args.description: UUID of the product this pricing is for.

args.name: pricing
args.description: Inline pricing configuration object. Either this or pricing_id must be provided.

args.name: product
args.description: Inline product definition object (name, description, sku, tax_codes, etc.).

args.name: start_date
args.description: Pricing start date in ISO 8601 format.

args.name: end_date
args.description: Pricing end date in ISO 8601 format.

args.name: anchor_date
args.description: Billing anchor date for this pricing.

args.name: external_id
args.description: External identifier for this pricing entry.

args.name: custom_data
args.description: Custom metadata for this pricing.

args.name: description
args.description: Description for this pricing entry.

args.name: plan_id
args.description: UUID of the plan template this pricing derives from.

tools.name: expireContract
tools.description: Expire an active contract. Only works on contracts with status 'active'. Optionally specify an expiry_date (defaults to today). The contract expires at end of day 23:59:59.999999.

args.name: contractId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the contract to expire.

args.name: expiry_date
args.description: Date to expire the contract (e.g. 2026-12-31). Defaults to today if not provided.

tools.name: voidInvoice
tools.description: Void an invoice by its ID. Only works on approved invoices that have not been fully paid. Voiding reverses the invoice and creates appropriate accounting entries.

args.name: invoiceId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the invoice to void.

tools.name: createInvoiceCreditNote
tools.description: Create a credit note against a specific invoice. The credit_note_amount is in the invoice's currency (e.g. 25 for $25). The API stores amounts in cents internally. Returns the created credit note.

args.name: invoiceId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the invoice to create a credit note against.

args.name: credit_note_amount
args.description: Amount for the credit note in the invoice's currency (e.g. 25 for $25.00).

args.name: reason
args.description: Reason for creating this credit note.

tools.name: generateInvoice
tools.description: Generate an invoice for a customer given a contract and date range. Use this for billing replay or on-demand invoice generation.

args.name: contract_id
args.description: UUID of the contract to generate the invoice for.

args.name: customer_id
args.description: UUID of the customer.

args.name: from_date
args.description: Start date of the billing period as a UNIX timestamp in seconds (e.g. 1709251200 for 2024-03-01). Must be an integer, NOT a date string.

args.name: to_date
args.description: End date of the billing period as a UNIX timestamp in seconds (e.g. 1711929600 for 2024-03-31). Must be an integer, NOT a date string.

tools.name: listCreditNotes
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of credit notes. Supports cursor-based pagination.

args.name: cursor
args.description: Cursor for pagination.

args.name: limit
args.description: Maximum number of results per page (defaults to 10).

args.name: customer_id
args.description: Filter by customer UUID.

args.name: invoice_id
args.description: Filter by invoice UUID.

args.name: status
args.description: Filter by status: in_progress, issued, void, or paid.

tools.name: getCreditNoteById
tools.description: Retrieve a credit note by its ID, including amount, status, customer, invoice association, and line items URL.

args.name: creditNoteId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the credit note.

tools.name: createPayment
tools.description: Record a payment. Link to invoices via payment_parts array. Payment method enum: card, cash, check, charge_back, bank_transfer, direct_debit, other, credits, etc. Type enum: payment, refund, payment_reversal, authorization, tax_withheld. For manual payments use payment_method='other' and type='payment'.

args.name: customer_id
args.description: UUID of the customer making the payment.

args.name: amount
args.description: Total payment amount in the smallest currency unit (e.g. cents).

args.name: currency_code
args.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code (e.g. USD).

args.name: payment_parts
args.description: Array of invoice allocations. Each element: {invoice_id: 'UUID', amount: 10000}. Links the payment to one or more invoices.

args.name: payment_method
args.description: Payment method: card, cash, check, charge_back, bank_transfer, direct_debit, other, credits, ach_credit_transfer, etc. Use 'other' for manual payments.

args.name: type
args.description: Payment type: payment, refund, payment_reversal, authorization, or tax_withheld.

args.name: status
args.description: Payment status.

args.name: external_id
args.description: External reference ID for this payment.

args.name: timestamp
args.description: Payment timestamp in ISO 8601 format.

tools.name: updatePayment
tools.description: Update a payment's invoice allocations. The API requires a non-empty request body with payment_parts.

args.name: paymentId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the payment to update.

args.name: payment_parts
args.description: Updated array of invoice allocations. Each element: {invoice_id: 'UUID', amount: 10000}. This field is required by the API.

tools.name: deleteManualPayment
tools.description: Delete a manual payment. Only works on payments that are in an eligible status for deletion.

args.name: paymentId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the payment to delete.

tools.name: refundPayment
tools.description: Refund a payment (full or partial). Specify the refund amount, destination, and whether to write off associated invoices.

args.name: paymentId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the payment to refund.

args.name: refund_amount
args.description: Amount to refund.

args.name: refund_destination
args.description: Where to send the refund: original_payment_method, credits, etc.

args.name: writeoff_invoices
args.description: Whether to write off the associated invoices (true/false).

tools.name: createInvoiceCharge
tools.description: Create an auto-charge for an invoice. Triggers payment collection via the customer's configured payment method.

args.name: invoiceId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the invoice to charge.

tools.name: editManualPayment
tools.description: Edit a manual payment's details (amount, method, etc.). Uses the manual_payment-specific endpoint.

args.name: paymentId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the manual payment to edit.

args.name: amount
args.description: Updated payment amount.

args.name: payment_method_details
args.description: Updated payment method details object.

args.name: payment_parts
args.description: Updated invoice allocations. Array of {invoice_id, amount}.

args.name: external_id
args.description: Updated external reference ID.

args.name: timestamp
args.description: Updated payment timestamp in ISO 8601 format.

tools.name: updateCustomer
tools.description: Update an existing customer's details. Supports partial updates — only send fields you want to change. Can update name, email, address, phone, communications_enabled, auto_charge_enabled, etc.

args.name: customerId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the customer to update.

args.name: customer_name
args.description: Updated customer name.

args.name: email
args.description: Updated email address.

args.name: phone_number
args.description: Updated phone number.

args.name: external_id
args.description: Updated external identifier.

args.name: address_line1
args.description: Address line 1.

args.name: address_line2
args.description: Address line 2.

args.name: address_city
args.description: City.

args.name: address_state
args.description: State.

args.name: address_zipCode
args.description: ZIP/postal code.

args.name: address_country
args.description: Country.

args.name: address_country_code
args.description: Country code (e.g. US, GB).

args.name: communications_enabled
args.description: Enable/disable invoice email communications.

args.name: auto_charge_enabled
args.description: Enable/disable auto-charge for this customer.

args.name: business_entity_id
args.description: UUID of the business entity to associate with.

tools.name: listProducts
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of products. Products define what is being sold and can be associated with pricing and contracts.

args.name: cursor
args.description: Cursor for pagination.

args.name: limit
args.description: Maximum number of results per page (defaults to 10).

args.name: name__ilike
args.description: Case-insensitive partial match for product name.

tools.name: getProductById
tools.description: Retrieve a product by its ID, including name, SKU, description, tax codes, pricing, and custom attributes.

args.name: productId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the product.

tools.name: getChartOfAccounts
tools.description: Retrieve the chart of accounts for the organization, listing all account categories and their hierarchical structure.

tools.name: listAccounts
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of accounting accounts (GL accounts) with optional filtering.

args.name: cursor
args.description: Cursor for pagination.

args.name: limit
args.description: Maximum number of accounts to return per page.

args.name: name__ilike
args.description: Case-insensitive partial match for account name.

tools.name: createAccount
tools.description: Create a new accounting (GL) account.

args.name: name
args.description: Name of the account.

args.name: account_category
args.description: Category of account. Valid values: 'Assets', 'Liabilities', 'Equity', 'Income', 'Expenses'.

args.name: description
args.description: Description of the account.

args.name: custom_data
args.description: Custom data object for the account.

tools.name: listJournalEntries
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of journal entries with optional filtering by date, account, or status.

args.name: cursor
args.description: Cursor for pagination.

args.name: limit
args.description: Maximum number of journal entries to return per page.

args.name: search_query
args.description: Search query to filter journal entries.

tools.name: createJournalEntry
tools.description: Create a new manual journal entry with debit and credit lines.

args.name: posted_at
args.description: Posting date of the journal entry in ISO 8601 format (e.g., '2026-01-15T00:00:00').

args.name: description
args.description: Description/memo for the journal entry.

args.name: currency
args.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP').

args.name: journal_lines
args.description: Array of journal lines. Each line requires: {account_id, debits, credits, currency}. Use 'debits' and 'credits' (plural, integers in cents). Example: [{account_id:'...', debits:10000, credits:0, currency:'USD'}, {account_id:'...', debits:0, credits:10000, currency:'USD'}]. Optional tags: customer_id, contract_id, invoice_id, custom_tags.

args.name: custom_data
args.description: Additional metadata as key-value pairs.

args.name: status_type
args.description: Journal entry status (default: 'posted').

tools.name: getJournalEntry
tools.description: Retrieve a journal entry by its ID, including all journal lines.

args.name: journalEntryId
args.description: The unique identifier of the journal entry.

tools.name: listJournalLines
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of journal lines (individual debit/credit entries) across all journal entries.

args.name: cursor
args.description: Cursor for pagination.

args.name: limit
args.description: Maximum number of journal lines to return per page.

args.name: search_query
args.description: Search query to filter journal lines.

tools.name: getBalanceSheet
tools.description: Retrieve the balance sheet report showing assets, liabilities, and equity.

args.name: start_date
args.description: Start date for the balance sheet period (YYYY-MM-DD).

args.name: end_date
args.description: End date for the balance sheet period (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to today.

tools.name: getIncomeStatement
tools.description: Retrieve the income statement (P&L) report showing revenue and expenses.

args.name: start_date
args.description: Start date for the income statement period (YYYY-MM-DD).

args.name: end_date
args.description: End date for the income statement period (YYYY-MM-DD).

tools.name: getAccountBalance
tools.description: Retrieve the balance for a specific accounting account.

args.name: accountId
args.description: The unique identifier of the account.

args.name: as_of
args.description: Date for the balance snapshot (YYYY-MM-DD).

tools.name: createProduct
tools.description: Create a new product in the catalog.

args.name: name
args.description: Name of the product.

args.name: sku
args.description: SKU (stock keeping unit) identifier.

args.name: description
args.description: Description of the product.

args.name: type
args.description: Product type. Valid values: 'product' (standard product) or 'group' (product group).

args.name: is_active
args.description: Whether the product is active.

args.name: tax_codes
args.description: Array of tax code objects for the product.

args.name: custom_attributes
args.description: Custom attributes as key-value pairs.

tools.name: updateProduct
tools.description: Update an existing product's details.

args.name: productId
args.description: The unique identifier of the product to update.

args.name: name
args.description: Updated name of the product.

args.name: sku
args.description: Updated SKU.

args.name: description
args.description: Updated description.

args.name: is_active
args.description: Whether the product is active.

args.name: tax_codes
args.description: Updated tax codes array.

args.name: custom_attributes
args.description: Updated custom attributes.

tools.name: getProductPricings
tools.description: Retrieve all pricing configurations for a specific product.

args.name: productId
args.description: The unique identifier of the product.

tools.name: createProductPricing
tools.description: Create a new pricing configuration for a product. The pricing_data object is a discriminated union — the 'pricing_type' field determines which pricing model schema to use.

args.name: productId
args.description: The unique identifier of the product.

args.name: name
args.description: Name of the pricing (optional).

args.name: description
args.description: Description of this pricing configuration.

args.name: pricing_data
args.description: Pricing data object (required). Must include 'pricing_type' discriminator. Supported types: flat_fee: {pricing_type:'flat_fee', unit_amount:10000}. per_unit: {pricing_type:'per_unit', unit_amount:500}. tiered: {pricing_type:'tiered', unit_amount:[1000,500], up_to:[100,null]}. volume: {pricing_type:'volume', unit_amount:[1000,500], up_to:[100,null]}. percent: {pricing_type:'percent', percentage:5.0}. package: {pricing_type:'package', package_size:10, unit_amount:5000}. step: {pricing_type:'step', unit_amount:[...], up_to:[...]}. matrix: {pricing_type:'matrix', dimensions:[...], values:[...]}.

args.name: billing_period
args.description: Billing cadence for this pricing. Object with 'cadence' (ISO 8601 duration, e.g. 'P1M' for monthly, 'P3M' for quarterly, 'P1Y' for annually) and optional 'offset'. Example: {cadence: 'P1M'}.

args.name: is_recurring
args.description: Whether this pricing is recurring (true) or one-time (false).

args.name: return_existing
args.description: If true, returns existing pricing with matching hash instead of creating a duplicate.

tools.name: listPlans
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of plans (templates v3) that define reusable contract structures.

args.name: cursor
args.description: Cursor for pagination.

args.name: limit
args.description: Maximum number of plans to return per page.

args.name: name__ilike
args.description: Case-insensitive partial match for plan name.

args.name: status
args.description: Filter by plan status. Valid values: draft, active, archived.

tools.name: getPlanById
tools.description: Retrieve a plan by its ID, including phases, products, and pricing configurations.

args.name: planId
args.description: The unique identifier of the plan.

tools.name: createPlan
tools.description: Create a new plan (template) that defines a reusable contract structure with products and pricing.

args.name: name
args.description: Name of the plan.

args.name: currency
args.description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP').

args.name: display_config
args.description: Customer-facing display content. Object with fields like title, description, etc.

args.name: duration
args.description: Billing period in ISO 8601 duration format (e.g., 'P1M' for monthly, 'P1Y' for yearly).

args.name: products
args.description: Array of products to add to plan, each with product_id and pricing configuration.

args.name: custom_attributes
args.description: Custom attributes as key-value pairs.

args.name: status
args.description: Plan status. Default: 'draft'. Options: 'draft', 'active', 'archived'.

args.name: business_entity_id
args.description: Business entity ID for invoicing.

tools.name: addProductsToPlan
tools.description: Add products with pricing to an existing plan.

args.name: planId
args.description: The unique identifier of the plan.

args.name: products
args.description: Array of product objects to add, each with product_id and pricing configuration.

tools.name: previewPlanEstimate
tools.description: Preview the estimated billing for a plan, showing projected charges per phase.

args.name: planId
args.description: The unique identifier of the plan.

args.name: start_date
args.description: Start date for the estimate (YYYY-MM-DD).

args.name: end_date
args.description: End date for the estimate (YYYY-MM-DD). Optional — calculated from plan duration if omitted.

tools.name: listCustomAttributes
tools.description: Retrieve a list of custom attribute definitions configured for the organization.

args.name: module
args.description: Filter by module. Valid values: 'customers', 'contracts', 'invoices', 'payments', 'products', 'plans'.

tools.name: createCustomAttribute
tools.description: Create a new custom attribute definition for an entity type.

args.name: attribute_key_name
args.description: Key name of the custom attribute (used as the field key).

args.name: display_name
args.description: Display name for the custom attribute.

args.name: module
args.description: Module/entity type this attribute applies to. Allowed values: 'customers', 'contracts', 'invoices', 'payments', 'products', 'plans'.

args.name: data_type
args.description: Data type of the attribute. Valid values: 'STRING', 'INTEGER', 'FLOAT', 'BOOLEAN', 'LIST_STRING', 'ENUM', 'JSON', 'DATE'.

args.name: default_value
args.description: Default value for the attribute.

args.name: enum_choices
args.description: Array of allowed values for enum-type attributes.

args.name: is_required
args.description: Whether the attribute is required.

args.name: description
args.description: Description of the custom attribute.

tools.name: listTaxCategories
tools.description: Retrieve a list of tax categories configured for the organization.

tools.name: createTaxCategory
tools.description: Create a new tax category.

args.name: name
args.description: Name of the tax category.

args.name: external_codes
args.description: Array of external tax code mappings (e.g., [{ "provider": "avalara", "code": "P0000000" }]).

tools.name: listJobs
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated list of async jobs (invoice generation, revenue recognition, etc.).

args.name: cursor
args.description: Cursor for pagination.

args.name: limit
args.description: Maximum number of jobs to return per page.

args.name: search
args.description: Search query to filter jobs. Format: 'field=value'.

tools.name: getJobById
tools.description: Retrieve a specific job by its ID to check status and results.

args.name: jobId
args.description: The unique identifier of the job.

tools.name: createContact
tools.description: Create a new contact associated with a customer.

args.name: customer
args.description: The customer ID (UUID) to associate this contact with.

args.name: first_name
args.description: First name of the contact.

args.name: last_name
args.description: Last name of the contact.

args.name: email
args.description: Email address of the contact.

args.name: send_contract
args.description: Whether to send contracts to this contact.

args.name: send_invoice
args.description: Whether to send invoices to this contact.

tools.name: updateContact
tools.description: Update an existing contact's details. Supports partial updates.

args.name: contactId
args.description: The unique identifier of the contact to update.

args.name: customer
args.description: The customer ID (UUID) this contact belongs to.

args.name: first_name
args.description: Updated first name.

args.name: last_name
args.description: Updated last name.

args.name: email
args.description: Updated email address.

args.name: send_contract
args.description: Whether to send contracts to this contact.

args.name: send_invoice
args.description: Whether to send invoices to this contact.

tools.name: listBusinessEntities
tools.description: Retrieve a list of business entities configured for the organization.

args.name: cursor
args.description: Cursor for pagination.

args.name: limit
args.description: Maximum number of entities to return.

tools.name: getBusinessEntityById
tools.description: Retrieve a business entity by its ID.

args.name: entityId
args.description: The unique identifier of the business entity.

tools.name: updateBusinessEntity
tools.description: Update an existing business entity's details.

args.name: entityId
args.description: The unique identifier of the business entity to update.

args.name: name
args.description: Updated name of the business entity.

args.name: address
args.description: Updated address object with line1, line2, city, state, zipCode, country, country_code.

args.name: tax_config
args.description: Tax configuration object (e.g., { "tax_id": "...", "tax_provider": "..." }).

args.name: email
args.description: Updated email address.

tools.name: listCustomerAddresses
tools.description: Retrieve all addresses for a specific customer.

args.name: customerId
args.description: The unique identifier of the customer.

tools.name: createCustomerAddress
tools.description: Add a new address to a customer.

args.name: customerId
args.description: The unique identifier of the customer.

args.name: line1
args.description: Address line 1.

args.name: line2
args.description: Address line 2.

args.name: city
args.description: City.

args.name: state
args.description: State/province.

args.name: zipCode
args.description: ZIP/postal code.

args.name: country
args.description: Country name.

args.name: country_code
args.description: ISO country code (e.g., 'US', 'GB').

args.name: line3
args.description: Address line 3.

args.name: is_default_billing
args.description: Set as default billing address. Default: false.

args.name: is_default_shipping
args.description: Set as default shipping address. Default: false.

tools.name: updateCustomerAddress
tools.description: Update an existing customer address.

args.name: customerId
args.description: The unique identifier of the customer.

args.name: addressId
args.description: The unique identifier of the address to update.

args.name: line1
args.description: Updated address line 1.

args.name: line2
args.description: Updated address line 2.

args.name: city
args.description: Updated city.

args.name: state
args.description: Updated state/province.

args.name: zipCode
args.description: Updated ZIP/postal code.

args.name: country
args.description: Updated country name.

args.name: country_code
args.description: Updated ISO country code.

args.name: line3
args.description: Updated address line 3.

args.name: is_default_billing
args.description: Set as default billing address.

args.name: is_default_shipping
args.description: Set as default shipping address.

tools.name: listPaymentMethods
tools.description: Retrieve all payment methods for a specific customer.

args.name: customerId
args.description: The unique identifier of the customer.

tools.name: attachPaymentMethod
tools.description: Attach a payment method to a customer.

args.name: customerId
args.description: The unique identifier of the customer.

args.name: payment_method_id
args.description: The payment method ID (from payment gateway) to attach.

args.name: connector
args.description: Payment connector name (e.g., 'stripe').

args.name: set_as_default
args.description: Whether to set as the default payment method.

tools.name: deleteCustomer
tools.description: Delete a customer by ID. This is permanent and cannot be undone. The customer must not have active contracts or unpaid invoices.

args.name: customerId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the customer to delete.

tools.name: deleteContact
tools.description: Delete a contact by ID.

args.name: contactId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the contact to delete.

tools.name: deletePaymentMethod
tools.description: Delete a payment method from a customer.

args.name: customerId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the customer.

args.name: paymentMethodId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the payment method to delete.

tools.name: pauseContract
tools.description: Pause an active contract. Requires a start_date for the pause and an unpause_extension_policy.

args.name: contractId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the contract to pause.

args.name: start_date
args.description: Date when the pause begins (ISO 8601 format, e.g. 2026-04-01T00:00:00).

args.name: unpause_extension_policy
args.description: How to handle the contract end date when unpaused.

args.name: end_date
args.description: Optional date when the pause ends and contract auto-resumes (ISO 8601).

tools.name: resumeContract
tools.description: Resume a paused contract. No request body needed.

args.name: contractId
args.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the contract to resume.
